hypothesis: Applying class-balancing strategies during training—specifically oversampling rare reaction templates to equalize their representation with frequent ones—will disproportionately improve out-of-distribution (OOD) top-10 accuracy on unseen templates for template-free models (MEGAN and RootAligned) compared to template-based models (LocalRetro), because template-free architectures implicitly learn generalizable chemical transformation patterns that are currently suppressed by majority-class dominance, whereas LocalRetro's explicit template library constrains it to predefined disconnections regardless of training balance.
